behaviors.description: Wait time between retries doubles with each consecutive failure, up to a configured maximum
behaviors.justification: Pure exponential backoff calculation logic with no I/O or external dependencies. Mocking retry count and max duration parameters is sufficient to verify the doubling formula and cap enforcement.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test parameterized across retry counts (1, 2, 3, N) and max cap values; verify wait times follow 2^N pattern and never exceed configured maximum

behaviors.description: Retry logic correctly identifies a rate-limit response and computes the appropriate wait time before the next attempt
behaviors.justification: Response identification and header parsing are deterministic operations. Mocked HTTP response objects with known status codes (429, 503) and standard headers (Retry-After) allow verification of identification logic and wait-time extraction.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test with mocked HTTP responses showing rate-limit status codes and various Retry-After header formats; verify library correctly identifies as rate-limit and extracts/computes the expected wait duration

behaviors.description: After the wait, the client actually resends the original request and returns the successful result to the caller transparently
behaviors.justification: Requires testing async flow where sleep must be awaited before resend occurs. Unit mocks might pass even if sleep is not awaited or async context is lost. Integration test with mocked HTTP client (429 then 200) and mocked sleep function verifies the full async promise chain executes correctly.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test mocking HTTP to return 429 first, then 200; mock sleep and verify it is awaited with correct duration; capture second HTTP request to confirm original request is resent; verify successful result returned to caller

behaviors.description: After the configured maximum number of retries, the client returns a typed QuotaExhaustedError rather than a generic error
behaviors.justification: Error type verification is synchronous, deterministic logic. Mocking HTTP to consistently return rate-limit allows exhaustion of retry budget and verification that the correct error type is thrown.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test that mocks HTTP client to always return 429; trigger max retry limit (e.g., max_retries=3); verify QuotaExhaustedError is thrown, not generic Error or other error types

behaviors.description: The full retry chain — initial call, backoff retries, quota tracking — terminates correctly and does not loop indefinitely before surfacing the error
behaviors.justification: Termination logic can be verified at unit level by mocking HTTP to always return rate-limit and using test timeout to detect hanging. Retry counter increment and limit-check logic are deterministic and testable via mocks.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test with test timeout (e.g., Jest timeout 5s) that mocks HTTP to always return 429; verify retry loop increments counter, checks against max_retries, and terminates after N attempts with error returned (not infinite hang)

behaviors.description: Requests issued concurrently from multiple async callers in the same process all draw from the same shared quota bucket
behaviors.justification: Cannot test shared state with a single caller (unit-level test impossible). Real async concurrency with Promise.all and multiple concurrent call() invocations required to verify quota bucket is shared, not duplicated per-caller or per-call.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test that issues 5+ concurrent async requests using Promise.all(call(), call(), ...); mock HTTP to return 429; verify all requests compete for the same quota counter and no caller gets independent quota allowance

behaviors.description: When multiple requests are waiting to be sent, they are dispatched in the order they were originally submitted (FIFO)
behaviors.justification: Queue ordering with multiple concurrent callers cannot be verified at unit level (single caller only). Integration test with multiple concurrent request submissions, request tracking on mock server side, and order assertion required.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test that submits 3+ concurrent requests with unique identifiers/payloads via Promise.all; mock HTTP server to track and timestamp request arrivals; verify requests are dispatched to server in FIFO order matching submission order
